title: anthyllis tenuicaulis
intro: Anthyllis tenuicaulis, also known as kidney vetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family that is commonly found in dry, rocky areas. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat a range of health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and other issues related to the urinary system, due to its diuretic and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Anthyllis tenuicaulis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
